title: Horizon: Temporal Aim as Foundational Grammar Primitive
description: Add temporal horizon to sd-core's Tension type, enabling horizon-aware dynamics computation (urgency, pressure, drift, relative recency), and expose it through werk-cli.

horizon-type.title: Horizon Type
horizon-type.description: New module: Horizon enum (Year, Month, Day, DateTime) with range computation, parsing, ordering, urgency, staleness, and full test suite.
expectedBehavior: Horizon enum with four variants: Year(i32), Month(i32, u32), Day(NaiveDate), DateTime(DateTime<Utc>)
range_start(), range_end(), width(), contains(), is_past() methods
Ord implementation: by range_start, ties broken by precision (narrower first)
parse(s: &str) -> Result<Horizon, HorizonParseError>: parse ISO-8601 partial dates (2026, 2026-05, 2026-05-15, 2026-05-15T14:00:00Z)
Display implementation: serialize to ISO-8601 partial date string
Serialize/Deserialize as ISO-8601 partial date string
urgency(created_at, now) -> f64: elapsed/total ratio, >1.0 when past horizon
staleness(last_mutation, now) -> f64: silence relative to horizon width
HorizonParseError error type
Self-contained module with no dependencies on other sd-core modules
verificationSteps: Parse all four variants from string and verify roundtrip
Range computation for each variant including edge cases (Feb 28/29, Dec 31, leap years)
Width computation matches expected durations
contains() returns true at boundaries, false outside
is_past() returns true when entire window elapsed, false otherwise
Ordering: Year < Month < Day < DateTime within same period; cross-period ordering correct
Staleness at various ratios
Invalid parse inputs produce clean HorizonParseError
Serde roundtrip preserves all variants

horizon-tension.title: Tension Struct Integration
horizon-tension.description: Add horizon field to Tension, new constructors, update_horizon method.
preconditions: horizon.rs module exists with Horizon type
expectedBehavior: Tension struct gains pub horizon: Option<Horizon> field
Tension::new() and Tension::new_with_parent() unchanged, horizon defaults to None
New constructor: Tension::new_full(desired, actual, parent_id, horizon) accepting all optional fields
Tension::update_horizon(&mut self, new_horizon: Option<Horizon>) -> Result<Option<Horizon>, SdError>
update_horizon rejected on non-Active tensions (returns InvalidStatusTransition or UpdateOnInactiveTension)
Past horizons allowed at creation time (grammar doesn't judge)
Serialization roundtrip preserves horizon field (present and absent)
verificationSteps: All existing tension tests pass unchanged (horizon = None by default)
new_full with each horizon variant creates correctly
update_horizon on Active tension succeeds and returns old value
update_horizon on Resolved/Released tension fails with appropriate error
JSON serialization roundtrip with horizon present
JSON serialization roundtrip with horizon absent (None)

horizon-mutation.title: Mutation Log Integration
horizon-mutation.description: Recognize 'horizon' as mutation field, support replay with horizon, extend creation format.
expectedBehavior: 'horizon' recognized as a valid mutation field alongside desired, actual, parent_id, status, created
apply_mutation handles 'horizon' arm: parse new_value as Horizon or empty string for None
ReconstructedTension gains pub horizon: Option<Horizon>, to_tension() updated
Creation mutation format extended: ";horizon='...'" appended when horizon present
parse_creation_value updated to extract horizon if present
Backward-compatible: creation mutations without horizon still parse correctly
verificationSteps: All existing mutation tests pass unchanged
Replay with horizon field mutation correctly updates tension
Replay creation mutation with horizon produces tension with horizon set
Replay creation mutation without horizon produces tension with horizon = None
Sequence: horizon set -> updated -> cleared via replay produces correct final state

horizon-store.title: Store Integration
horizon-store.description: Schema change (horizon TEXT column), CRUD operations, migration, query updates.
expectedBehavior: tensions table schema gains 'horizon TEXT' column (nullable)
Migration: ALTER TABLE tensions ADD COLUMN horizon TEXT for existing databases
create_tension_full(desired, actual, parent_id, horizon) method
update_horizon(id, new_horizon: Option<Horizon>) method following existing update_desired/update_actual pattern
All query methods (get_tension, list_tensions, get_children, get_roots, parse_tension_rows) return horizon
persist_tension includes horizon column
Mutation recorded when horizon changes
Tension construction in all query paths includes horizon field
verificationSteps: All existing store tests pass unchanged
Create tension with horizon, retrieve, verify horizon present
Create tension without horizon, retrieve, verify horizon = None
Update horizon, verify mutation recorded in mutation log
list_tensions/get_children/get_roots all return horizon correctly
Migration: simulate opening existing DB without horizon column (verify ALTER succeeds)

horizon-events.title: Event System Integration
horizon-events.description: HorizonChanged event variant, updated TensionCreated event, EventBuilder methods.
preconditions: Store has update_horizon and create_tension_full methods
expectedBehavior: New Event variant: HorizonChanged { tension_id, old_horizon: Option<String>, new_horizon: Option<String>, timestamp }
TensionCreated event gains horizon: Option<String> field
EventBuilder::horizon_changed(...) method
EventBuilder::tension_created(...) updated to include horizon
Store's update_horizon emits HorizonChanged event
Store's create_tension_full emits TensionCreated with horizon
verificationSteps: All existing event tests pass unchanged
HorizonChanged event emitted when horizon updated via store
TensionCreated event includes horizon when present
TensionCreated event has horizon=None when absent
Event serialization roundtrip for HorizonChanged
Subscriber receives horizon events

horizon-dynamics-new.title: Dynamics — Urgency, Pressure, Drift
horizon-dynamics-new.description: New dynamics types and functions: Urgency, HorizonDrift, temporal pressure, pressure field on StructuralTension.
preconditions: Horizon type exists with urgency() and staleness() methods
expectedBehavior: Urgency struct: tension_id, value (f64, 0.0 to unbounded), time_remaining (seconds), total_window (seconds)
compute_urgency(tension: &Tension, now) -> Option<Urgency>: returns None when no horizon
HorizonDrift struct: tension_id, drift_type, change_count, net_shift_seconds
HorizonDriftType enum: Stable, Tightening, Postponement, RepeatedPostponement, Oscillating
detect_horizon_drift(tension_id, mutations) -> HorizonDrift: pattern detection on horizon mutations
compute_temporal_pressure(tension, now) -> Option<f64>: magnitude * urgency
StructuralTension gains pub pressure: Option<f64> field
compute_structural_tension updated to compute pressure when horizon present
verificationSteps: Urgency at 0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%, 150% (past horizon)
Urgency returns None when tension has no horizon
Pressure = magnitude * urgency (within tolerance)
Pressure = None when no horizon
Horizon drift: stable (no horizon changes)
Horizon drift: single postponement
Horizon drift: repeated postponement (3+ shifts later)
Horizon drift: tightening (net shift earlier or higher precision)
Horizon drift: oscillation (back and forth)
All existing compute_structural_tension tests pass (pressure = None when no horizon)

horizon-dynamics-relative.title: Dynamics — Horizon-Relative Recency
horizon-dynamics-relative.description: Internal horizon-relative threshold scaling across all existing dynamics functions. Largest bead — touches every dynamics function.
preconditions: Urgency, HorizonDrift, temporal pressure all implemented
expectedBehavior: Internal helper: effective_recency(absolute_recency, horizon, now) -> i64 scaling recency to horizon width
detect_structural_conflict: uses effective_recency + detects temporal crowding (siblings with overlapping narrow horizons)
detect_oscillation: uses effective_recency + detects temporal oscillation from horizon mutations
detect_resolution: computes required_velocity = remaining_gap / time_remaining when horizon present
classify_creative_cycle_phase: horizon-relative phase boundaries (germination = early in window, etc.)
detect_neglect: urgency-weighted attention asymmetry between parent and children
measure_assimilation_depth: frequency thresholds scaled by horizon width
detect_compensating_strategy: persistence threshold scaled by horizon width
predict_structural_tendency: urgency factored into prediction
All functions: when horizon is None, behavior identical to current (regression safety)
verificationSteps: Each function tested with and without horizon
Without horizon: output identical to current behavior (regression tests)
Conflict: temporal crowding detected when siblings share narrow horizon
Conflict: no temporal crowding when siblings have wide/different horizons
Oscillation: temporal oscillation detected from horizon mutation pattern
Resolution: 'sufficient velocity' computed against horizon
Lifecycle: phase classification shifts appropriately with urgency
Neglect: asymmetry weighted by child urgency
Assimilation depth: relative frequency scales with horizon width
Compensating strategy: persistence threshold scales with horizon width
Structural tendency: urgency biases prediction toward advancing or release

horizon-tree.title: Tree Integration
horizon-tree.description: Horizon-aware queries on Forest: children sorted by horizon, past-horizon and approaching-horizon filters.
expectedBehavior: Forest::children_by_horizon(parent_id) -> Vec<&Node>: children sorted by horizon (earliest range_start first), None last
Forest::tensions_past_horizon(now) -> Vec<&Node>: all active tensions whose horizon window has fully elapsed
Forest::tensions_approaching_horizon(now, within: Duration) -> Vec<&Node>: active tensions whose horizon ends within given duration
verificationSteps: All existing tree tests pass unchanged
Children sorted correctly: earliest horizon first, no-horizon last
Past-horizon query returns only active tensions with elapsed horizons, not resolved/released
Approaching-horizon query returns correct set for various durations
Empty results when no horizons set on any tension
Mixed horizon/no-horizon children sort correctly

horizon-engine.title: Engine Integration
horizon-engine.description: Urgency and drift in the dynamics computation cycle, engine-level create/update methods.
preconditions: Horizon-relative recency implemented in all dynamics functions
expectedBehavior: DynamicsEngine::compute_and_emit_for_tension computes urgency and horizon drift
DynamicsEngine::create_tension_full method accepting Option<Horizon>
DynamicsEngine::update_horizon method for changing tension horizon
PreviousDynamics gains had_urgency_above_threshold: bool for transition events
Urgency and drift results included in dynamics computation output
verificationSteps: All existing engine tests pass unchanged
Engine computes urgency for tensions with horizon
Engine returns None urgency for tensions without horizon
Engine detects horizon drift from mutation history
Full cycle: create tension with horizon -> update actual -> compute dynamics -> verify urgency and pressure in results
Engine create_tension_full stores horizon correctly
Engine update_horizon records mutation and emits event

horizon-cli.title: werk-cli Integration
horizon-cli.description: CLI commands and display: --horizon flag on add, werk horizon command, show/tree/context display.
expectedBehavior: werk add gains --horizon flag (e.g. --horizon 2026-05)
New command: werk horizon <id> <value> to set horizon (e.g. werk horizon abc1 2026-05)
werk horizon <id> none clears horizon
werk horizon <id> (no value) displays current horizon with urgency
werk show displays Horizon line with human-readable interpretation and days remaining
werk show --verbose displays urgency, pressure, staleness, horizon drift
werk tree sorts siblings by horizon (earliest first, no-horizon last)
werk tree displays horizon annotation in brackets [2026-05] or [—] for no horizon
werk context JSON includes horizon, horizon_range, urgency, pressure, staleness_ratio
Invalid horizon strings produce clear error messages with examples
verificationSteps: All existing CLI tests pass unchanged
werk add --horizon 2026-05 creates tension with horizon set
werk add without --horizon creates tension with horizon = None
werk horizon <id> 2026-05 sets horizon, mutation recorded
werk horizon <id> none clears horizon
werk horizon <id> displays current horizon and urgency
werk show displays horizon line
werk show --verbose includes urgency, pressure, drift
werk tree sorts siblings correctly by horizon
werk tree displays horizon annotations
werk context JSON includes all horizon fields
Invalid horizon (e.g. 'abc', '2026-13', '2026-05-32') produces helpful error

criticalPath: horizon-type -> horizon-tension -> horizon-mutation -> horizon-store -> horizon-events -> horizon-cli

executionOrder.note: Parallel: store path, dynamics path, tree path
Parallel: store path completes, dynamics path completes
CLI integration — all sd-core work complete
